message:
fix(fcp-oauth): make authorization_header fallible when token material is missing

Previously OAuthTokens::authorization_header() returned a String unconditionally
via format!("{} {}", self.token_type, self.access_token). An OAuthTokens value
constructed from a degenerate TokenResponse (empty access_token or empty
token_type) would happily produce a string like " tok" or "Bearer " — which is
a silently broken Authorization header that downstream HTTP clients will send
over the wire and fail to authenticate with in ways that are hard to trace back
to the token store.

Change authorization_header() to return OAuthResult<String>:
  - access_token empty  -> Err(InvalidTokenResponse("stored token is missing access_token"))
  - token_type   empty  -> Err(InvalidTokenResponse("stored token is missing token_type"))
  - otherwise           -> Ok(format!("{token_type} {access_token}"))

Callers now get a typed error at the point of use instead of a malformed header
arriving at the remote server. This keeps the error taxonomy already documented
on OAuthError (InvalidTokenResponse) and drops the #[must_use] annotation since
Result is inherently #[must_use].

Update all in-crate call sites:
  - crates/fcp-oauth/src/token.rs unit tests: .expect(...) on the happy paths,
    matches!(err, OAuthError::InvalidTokenResponse(_)) on the empty-field paths.
  - crates/fcp-oauth/tests/no_mock_integration.rs: same .expect(...) pattern for
    the two assertions that previously compared the raw String.

Add a new unit test (test_token_authorization_header_empty_access_token_errors)
pinning the empty-access-token branch alongside the existing empty-token-type
regression at token.rs:1662+.

Breaking change for any downstream caller of authorization_header() - grep the
workspace before upgrading. No callers inside this repo besides the ones
updated here (verified via ripgrep).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
